pages router site - standalone nextjs site - content audit - duplicate content - section overlap - page duplication --- # Professional-Services Website (Architecture A) Build a standalone Next.js 15 site for a professional services firm (consultancy, law firm, advisory, B2B supplier) with dual-audience routing or single-audience lead gen. **Two distinct sub-types:** | Sub-type | Example | Content Pattern | CTA Pattern | |----------|---------|----------------|-------------| | **B2C Advisory** | Golden Visa, Law Firm | Dual-path (investor/business), testimonials, comparison tables | Book a call, contact form | | **B2B Lead Gen** | Glass supplier, industrial products | Technical specs, project portfolio, certifications | "Request quote" → WhatsApp, quote form | ## When to Use This vs the Builder | Criteria | Use Standalone (Arch A) | Use Builder (Arch B) | |---|---|---| | Dual-path routing (investor vs business) | ✅ | ❌ (single flow) | | Age-gate / path-choice modal | ✅ | ❌ | | Complex comparison tables | ✅ | Limited | | FAQ with multi-tab switching | ✅ | Limited | | 5+ custom pages | ✅ | ❌ (builder does 1 page) | | Team has 3+ staff profiles | ✅ | ✅ | | Simple brochure (home + contact) | ❌ | ✅ | | B2B with technical product catalog | ✅ | ❌ | | Quote request form | ✅ | ❌ | | No prices (custom quoting) | ✅ N/A | ✅ N/A | --- ## Sub-type A: B2C Advisory (Golden Visa Pattern) See the original implementation in `references/golden-visa-mvp-pattern.md`. Key traits: - Entry modal (language + path selection) - Investor and Business landing paths - Testimonials, trust signals, team profiles - Dark theme with gold accents --- ## Sub-type B: B2B Lead Gen (Pitchy Pattern) Build a B2B site from the `Ai-Whisperers/template-nextjs-client` template. This template is e-commerce B2C by default — you must transform it. ### Transformation Checklist (Remove B2C, Add B2B) **REMOVE these from the cloned template:** - `app/tienda/` — product store with cart - `app/envio/` — shipping info - `app/promociones/` — promotions - `app/producto/[slug]/` — individual product pages (replace with /productos/ catalog) - `components/search-autocomplete.tsx` — no product search needed - `components/dark-mode-toggle.tsx` — B2B stays light theme - `components/payment-methods.tsx` — no payments - `components/quick-order.tsx` — no quick ordering - `components/recently-viewed.tsx` — no recently viewed - `components/product-card.tsx` — replace with B2B product card (no price) - `components/hero-carousel.tsx` — single hero image for B2B - `components/promo-carousel.tsx` — no promotions - `components/bottom-nav.tsx` — not needed - `lib/wishlist.ts` and `lib/recently-viewed.ts` — not needed - `lib/hooks/use-wishlist.ts`, `lib/hooks/use-recently-viewed.ts` — remove, update `lib/hooks/index.ts` - `public/images/products/` and `public/images/categories/` — template placeholder images - `public/manifest.json`, `public/sw.js` — PWA files from template **CREATE these new pages:** - `app/productos/page.tsx` — Product catalog with specs, no prices, "Request quote" per item - `app/proyectos/page.tsx` — Portfolio gallery - `app/servicios/page.tsx` — Service listing with process steps - `app/cotizar/page.tsx` — Quote request form → WhatsApp ### @ai-whisperers Package Version Mapping The template pins `^0.1.0` but the GitHub Packages registry has newer versions. When `npm install` fails with `ETARGET` or `401`, check and set exact versions: ```bash # Check available versions (requires GITHUB_TOKEN in env) GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) npm view @ai-whisperers/admin version ``` | Package | Pinned | Actual latest (as of May 2026) | |---------|--------|-------------------------------| | `@ai-whisperers/ui` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.4.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/theme` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/auth` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/commerce` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/admin` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.1` | | `@ai-whisperers/whatsapp` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/seo` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/i18n` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | Remove `@ai-whisperers/test-vitest` from devDependencies — it doesn't exist in the registry. ### Content Structure for Blog The template's blog components expect `blog.posts[]` (array under a posts key). If your content JSON has `blog` as a flat array, you must adapt the components: ```tsx // In app/blog/page.tsx — adapt data structure const b = { posts: (c.blog || []), categories: [...new Set((c.blog || []).map((p: any) => p.category).filter(Boolean))], title: "Blog", subtitle: "" } // In app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx const post = ((c.blog || []) as any[]).find((p: any) => p.slug === slug) ``` ### B2B Theme Config Use a light theme with blue/cyan palette for construction/industrial B2B: ```json { "palettes": { "default": { "primary": "#0F62FE", "primaryForeground": "#FFFFFF", "secondary": "#1A1A2E", "secondaryForeground": "#E2E8F0", "accent": "#00A3E0", "background": "#FFFFFF", "surface": "#F8F9FA", "surfaceLight": "#E8ECF0", "text": "#1A1A2E", "textLight": "#4A5568", "textMuted": "#6B7280" } }, "defaultPalette": "default", "theme": "light" } ``` ### SEO Strategy for Zero-Competition Keywords For B2B industrial/construction sites in Paraguay, search for keywords with ZERO existing competition. The market analysis repo has these patterns: ```bash # Find keywords with no competing pages "muro cortina Paraguay" → no rankable results "vidrio fachada Asunción" → no rankable results "vidrio templado Paraguay" → low competition ``` Write 3 blog posts targeting these before launch. Post schedule: one per day for the first 3 days. ### Standard B2B Pages | Page | Purpose | Content | |------|---------|---------| | `/` | Hero + features + product overview + CTA | B2B value props, trust signals | | `/productos` | Technical product catalog | Specs, applications, no prices, "Request quote" CTA per item | | `/proyectos` | Portfolio gallery | Project photos, scope, client names | | `/servicios` | Service listing | Process steps, what each service includes | | `/cotizar` | Quote request form | Name, company, email, phone, project type, product selection, m2, timeline → WhatsApp | | `/nosotros` | Company story | Stats, team, certifications, values | | `/blog` | SEO content | Technical articles, buyer education | | `/contacto` | Contact | Form + phone + WhatsApp + map | | `/faq` | FAQ | Common customer questions | --- ## Sub-type C: Pages Router Standalone (Nexa Paraguay Pattern) A Pages Router Next.js site deployed as a standalone Docker image, with a single locale (ES) and content-driven sections. Images are referenced in `images.json` and resolved at runtime. Used when the client needs a lighter-weight site than the full App Router architecture. ### Architecture ``` repo-root/ ├── src/pages/ │ ├── index.tsx # homepage — getServerSideProps loads content + images │ ├── [slug].tsx # all other pages — SLUG_MAP routes slugs to page configs │ └── admin/content.tsx # optional CMS page ├── src/components/ │ ├── sections.tsx # SECTION_MAP with HeroSection, ProgramsSection, etc. │ ├── content.ts # resolveContent(), resolveImage() helpers │ ├── Header.tsx # renders logo + nav │ └── Footer.tsx ├── nexa-pages/ # page config JSON files (home.json, servicios.json, etc.) ├── content/ │ └── es.json # all site content (single locale) ├── images.json # image manifest — all image paths resolved through this ├── public/images/ # actual image files (brand/, hero/, programs/, etc.) ├── Dockerfile # must include images.json copying at runtime stage ├── docker-compose.yml # Traefik labels at service-level, NOT deploy: labels └── package.json # may use file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/... local deps ``` ### Image Resolution Pattern Content uses `"@src:trust.certificate"` or `"$img": "programs.tierBase"` syntax referencing keys in `images.json`. A helper resolves these at runtime: ```typescript // src/components/content.ts export function resolveImage(images: any, ref: string): string { if (!ref || !images) return '' if (ref.startsWith('@img:') || ref.startsWith('@src:')) { const key = ref.startsWith('@img:') ? ref.replace('@img:', '') : ref.replace('@src:', '') const parts = key.split('.') let obj = images for (const p of parts) { if (obj?.[p]) obj = obj[p] else return '' } return obj?.src || obj?.fallbackSrc || '' } return ref } ``` ### Critical: images.json Must Be in Docker Image The Dockerfile does NOT copy the entire repo — only specific dirs. You MUST add `images.json` explicitly: ```dockerfile COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/images.json ./images.json # ← REQUIRED COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/nexa-pages ./nexa-pages ``` Without this line, `loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')` returns null at runtime and every image reference resolves to empty string — no images render, no error message. ### Passing Images to Section Components Both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` must: 1. Load `images.json` in `getServerSideProps`: ```typescript const images = loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')?.images || {} ``` 2. Pass it to the return object: ```typescript return { props: { content, pageConfig, images, pageId: slug, timestamp: Date.now() } } ``` 3. Pass it to section components AND reconstruct pageContent properly: ```tsx if (Component) { const sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id) const pc: any = {} const keyName = section.content?.split('.').pop() || section.id if (section.content || section.id) pc[keyName] = sectionData || content return } ``` **Why this matters**: Old `sections.tsx` components access data via `pageContent.hero`, `pageContent.stats`, `pageContent.trust` — they expect the key name to be the LAST segment of the content key (`home.hero` → `hero`). Without this reconstruction using `.split('.').pop()`, the pageContent would use `section.id` (e.g. `"stats-counter"`) which doesn't match what the component looks for (`"stats"`), and ALL section data would be invisible despite the component rendering. **This must be applied identically in both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` (they are near-duplicates).** If `package.json` has `"@ai-whisperers/admin": "file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/..."`, the Docker build fails because the `file:` path doesn't exist inside the container. **Fix:** Build on the VPS host first (`npm install && npm run build`), then build Docker from the already-ready `node_modules`. The `COPY . .` in the Dockerfile picks up the existing `node_modules` directory, and `npm install --legacy-peer-deps` runs against the already-installed deps. This works because npm skips re-installation for packages it can't resolve via `file:` when they're already in `node_modules`. ### SVG Logo Fallback When the client has no designer-provided SVG logo, create one inline: ```svg ``` Create three variants: - `logo.svg` — standard (navy on white background) - `logo-dark.svg` — white text for dark backgrounds (footer) - `logo-icon.svg` — icon-only mark Reference in `images.json`: ```json "logo": { "src": "/images/brand/logo.webp", "srcSvg": "/images/brand/logo.svg", ... } ``` Update `Header.tsx` to render `` instead of plain text `{businessName}`. ### Pitfalls Specific to Pages Router Standalone For a complete deploy checklist covering ALL items in this section plus verification steps, see `references/client-site-launch-checklist.md`. For the section component building pattern (FAQ, Blog, Team, Privacy, Glossary, etc.), see `references/section-components-pattern.md`. For theme standardisation and design-token extraction, see the `src/theme.ts` pattern described below. For a comprehensive code-quality audit checklist covering the 40+ issues found in a production Pages Router site, see `references/pages-router-audit-checklist.md`. - **Theme standardisation**: After building section components, extract all hardcoded hex colors, border-radius values, and spacing into a single `src/theme.ts` file: ```typescript export const theme = { colors: { primary: '#1B2A4A', accent: '#C9A96E', bg: '#F5F5F0', text: '#444', muted: '#666', border: '#e0e0e0' }, radii: { sm: '8px', md: '12px', lg: '16px', full: '50px' }, shadows: { card: '0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)' }, fonts: { body: "'Inter', sans-serif", heading: "'Playfair Display', serif" }, spacing: { section: '4rem 1rem', card: '1.5rem', btn: '0.85rem 2.5rem' }, sizes: { maxWidth: '1200px', contentWidth: '800px' }, } ``` Import in components: `import { theme } from '../theme'` then `style={{ background: theme.colors.primary }}`. Destructure frequently-used keys at top of file: `const c = theme.colors, r = theme.radii, s = theme.spacing`. - **Mobile navigation**: The hamburger button in Header.tsx is often hidden (`display: none`) in the initial scaffold. Enable it with a `@media (max-width: 768px)` CSS-in-JS block that toggles nav visibility via React state: ```tsx ``` - **Footer social links**: site.json typically has `social: { linkedin, instagram }` but the Footer component doesn't render them. Add `socialLinks` to the footer content object and render as `` tags with the site.json social URLs. - **Cache headers for SSR pages**: `getServerSideProps` reads JSON from disk on every request. Add caching via `res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'public, s-maxage=60, stale-while-revalidate=300')` or return a `headers` key from `getServerSideProps` (Next.js 13+ supports returning `headers` from `getServerSideProps`). - **Navigation hrefs**: The content may use multi-tenant paths like `/s/es/nexa-paraguay/programas`. For standalone deployment, replace with plain paths: `/programas`. Check ALL occurrences in ALL content JSON files (es, en, nl, de) — nav item hrefs, hero CTA links, hero CTA secondary links, blog-related URLs, and ANY href field. A missed multiclient path in content/en.json nav hrefs causes EVERY nav link in the EN locale to 404. The Header component must prepend the locale prefix to nav hrefs (e.g., `/servicios` → `/en/servicios`) after stripping any remaining multiclient prefix. Use a grep across all locale files: `grep -rn '/s/' content/*.json`. - **Placeholder template variables**: Content may have `{{taxRate}}` placeholders. If the component doesn't replace them, pre-replace in the content JSON with the actual value. Search across ALL pages content, not just home. - **Blog post links must include locale prefix**: Blog "Leer más" / "Read more" links in the BlogSection component must prepend the current locale. Without this, blog links on EN/NL/DE pages navigate to `/blog/post-slug` (no locale) which hits the catch-all route without the locale prefix, causing a 404 or wrong-content render. Fix: use `useRouter` from `next/router` and prepend: `href={\`/${locale}/blog/${post.slug}\`}`. The locale variable comes from the page component's `locale` prop, NOT from `router.locale` (which may be undefined in Pages Router). - **Content JSON corruption from patch tool**: The `patch` tool can corrupt large JSON files (208KB, 900+ lines) by misplacing commas. A missing comma between `}` and `\"seo\"` breaks the entire site — every page SSR returns `content: {}` with zero Docker errors. **Always validate after every JSON edit**: `python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('content/es.json')); print('VALID')\"`. For safety, prefer Python manipulation: `json.load()` + `json.dump()` instead of `patch` or `sed` on large JSON files. Add pre-deploy validation: `for f in content/*.json; do python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('$f'))\" && echo \"$f: VALID\" || exit 1; done`. - **Docker service labels at service-level**: In `docker-compose.yml`, Traefik labels must be at `services.web.deploy.labels` level, NOT at `services.web.labels`. Docker Compose standalone ignores `deploy:` labels. - **Content items use q/a keys**: FAQ, privacy, and glossary items in content JSON may use `q`/`a` keys instead of `pregunta`/`respuesta` or `question`/`answer` or `term`/`definition`. Support ALL variants in component logic: `item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title` and `item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body`. Prefer `q`/`a` first since that's the most common format in the content source. - **Hero CTA hrefs share the same problem**: Nav hrefs aren't the only place multiclient paths hide. Hero CTAs (`home.hero.ctaPrimaryHref`, `home.hero.ctaSecondaryHref`) in EN/NL/DE locale files also use `/s/en/nexa-paraguay/` paths. These cause the hero buttons on non-ES locales to 404. Run a systematic grep: `grep -rn 'cta.*Href' content/*.json | grep '/s/'`. - **Section components receive resolved data directly**: When dispatching to SECTION_MAP, pass `resolveContent(content, section.content)` as `pageContent`, NOT the full page build result. Otherwise the component needs to dig through intermediary keys. Accept both `pageContent` (direct data) and `data` (backward-compat) in component props. - **SECTION_MAP must be in both index.tsx and [slug].tsx**: Both files dispatch section components. Always add new entries to both. - **Rich fallback for [slug].tsx**: The generic section renderer in `[slug].tsx` must handle more than just `headline`/`subheadline`. Add eyebrow text, body content paragraphs, and item grids (for FAQ, glossary, services, blog posts, team members). The renderer should detect `data.items`, `data.groups`, `data.pillars` arrays and render them as card grids. Each item card shows `title`/`pregunta`/`question`/`term` + `description`/`respuesta`/`answer`/`definition`/`body`. - **buildPageContent removal breaks section rendering**: Removing `buildPageContent` is recommended (it's redundant once the dispatch passes sectionData directly), BUT you must reconstruct `pageContent` with the correct key name. Section components in `sections.tsx` access data via `pageContent.hero` — they expect the content key's LAST segment (`home.hero` → `hero`), NOT the section id (`hero` happens to match but `stats-counter` doesn't match `stats`). The dispatch must use `section.content?.split('.').pop()` as the pageContent key. Without this, ALL section data appears invisible despite the component rendering — the hero background image won't load, trust/programs/process images won't appear, and stats won't show values. See the "Passing Images to Section Components" section above for the correct dispatch code. - **Every page config needs SLUG_MAP entry**: If a new page config JSON is added to `nexa-pages/`, it MUST also be added to `SLUG_MAP` in `[slug].tsx` or it will 404. The `SLUG_MAP` is the routing layer between URL slugs and page config filenames. - **image paths in images.json**: After copying `images.json` from the paragu-ai-builder source, ALL paths use `/sites/nexa-paraguay/images/...` prefix which only works in the multi-tenant context. For standalone deployment, batch-replace to `/images/...`. Use a Python script to walk the JSON and fix all `src`, `fallbackSrc`, `fallback`, and `basePath` fields. - **Image path verification**: After fixing paths, verify every referenced image exists on disk with a script. The `images.json` may reference images that don't exist yet. Run `python3 -c "..."` to compare referenced src paths against actual files in `public/images/`. - **Docker service update may not deploy new config**: After `docker build` and `docker service update --image`, the new container may still have old content JSON due to Docker build cache layers. Always verify inside the container: `docker exec $(docker ps --filter name= -q) cat /app/nexa-pages/home.json | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print([s['id'] for s in d['sections']])"`. If old, force a restart: `docker service update --force `. - **Content duplication across pages is common**: Pages Router section-driven sites easily accumulate duplicate content when page configs share section IDs or content paths point to identical data. Always run a systematic scan (see `references/content-duplication-audit-pattern.md`) before deploying new pages. ## Deployment (Both Sub-types) ```yaml # docker-compose.yml services: web: build: . image: :prod ports: ["3000"] networks: [agent-net] deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.http.routers..entrypoints=websecure" - "traefik.http.routers..rule=Host(`.paragu-ai.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers..tls=true" - "traefik.http.routers..tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver" - "traefik.http.services..loadbalancer.server.port=3000" - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net" healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 15s networks: agent-net: external: true ``` **Important:** The template's docker-compose.yml will have hardcoded old values (site name, image name, Traefik rules). Update ALL of them for the new site. ### CI/CD Registration After pushing the repo, add it to ci-cd's `clients.json`: ```json "": { "site": "", "repo": "Ai-Whisperers/", "url": "https://.paragu-ai.com", "type": "nextjs", "pm": "npm", "node": "20", "docker_image": ":prod", "docker_service": "_web", "deploy_strategy": "build-and-scp" } ``` ### DNS (Cloudflare) Add A record in Cloudflare dashboard for paragu-ai.com: - Type: A - Name: `` - Content: `72.61.44.159` - Proxy: Proxied No Cloudflare API key is available in this environment — DNS changes must be done through the Cloudflare dashboard. ## Project Structure (Common) ``` client-site/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # providers + metadata + fonts │ ├── page.tsx # home page │ ├── productos/ # (B2B) product catalog │ ├── proyectos/ # (B2B) portfolio │ ├── servicios/ # (B2B) services │ ├── cotizar/ # (B2B) quote form │ ├── blog/ # blog index + [slug] │ ├── nosotros/ # about page │ ├── contacto/ # contact form │ └── faq/ # FAQ accordion ├── components/ # reusable UI components ├── content/ │ ├── es.json # all site content │ └── tokens.json # design tokens ├── Dockerfile ├── docker-compose.yml └── package.json ``` ## Content Management All site copy lives in `content/es.json` (single file). Structure per site type: ### B2B content sections ```json { "home": { "hero": {}, "features": {}, "finalCta": {} }, "products": [{ id, name, description, features[], applications[], cta }], "proyectos": [{ name, location, scope, image }], "servicios": [{ id, title, description, icon }], "nosotros": { story, stats[], values[] }, "faq": [{ question, answer }], "blog": [{ slug, title, excerpt, content }] } ``` ## Multi-Locale Routing (Activation Pattern) All four locales (`es`, `en`, `nl`, `de`) should be activated as infrastructure, not just the default. The pattern: ### Middleware Locale Detection **CRITICAL: Cookie name must match getServerSideProps.** The middleware reads `NEXT_LOCALE` cookie, and getServerSideProps sets it as `NEXT_LOCALE`. If they disagree, Accept-Language header overrides on every visit. Also check `request.cookies.get('locale')` for backward compatibility with any previous middleware version. - **Factory-demo content ships by default**: Fabricated stats (+500 families, 98% success), AI headshots, fake testimonials — these are NOT placeholders the client filled in, they're the scaffold. Replace them before anyone sees the site. Check `is_demo` flag in site.json. // src/middleware.ts const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] const DEFAULT_LOCALE = 'es' export function middleware(request: NextRequest) { const { pathname } = request.nextUrl const firstSegment = pathname.split('/')[1] const hasLocale = LOCALES.includes(firstSegment) if (!hasLocale && !pathname.startsWith('/_next') && !pathname.startsWith('/images') && pathname !== '/favicon.svg') { // Read NEXT_LOCALE first (set by getServerSideProps), fall back to 'locale' (legacy) let preferred = request.cookies.get('NEXT_LOCALE')?.value || request.cookies.get('locale')?.value || DEFAULT_LOCALE if (!LOCALES.includes(preferred)) { const acceptLang = request.headers.get('accept-language') || '' for (const loc of LOCALES) { if (acceptLang.startsWith(loc) || acceptLang.includes(` ${loc}`) || acceptLang.includes(`;${loc}`)) { preferred = loc; break } } } const url = new URL(`/${preferred}${pathname}`, request.url) if (request.nextUrl.search) url.search = request.nextUrl.search return NextResponse.redirect(url, 302) } const result = NextResponse.next() return result } export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next|images|favicon\\.svg).*)'], } ``` ### Locale Detection in getServerSideProps — URL Prefix MUST Win **This is the most common multi-locale bug.** The URL prefix (`/es/servicios`) must ALWAYS override cookie and Accept-Language. Without tracking whether the URL explicitly specified a locale, a stale `NEXT_LOCALE=en` cookie will override `/es/`: ```typescript export function getServerSideProps({ params, req, res }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' let hasUrlLocale = false const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] // 1) URL prefix locale takes highest priority if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; hasUrlLocale = true slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } else if (LOCALES.includes(slug)) { locale = slug; hasUrlLocale = true; slug = 'home' } // 2) If no URL locale, check cookie (only if URL didn't specify) if (!hasUrlLocale) { const cookieLocale = req?.cookies?.NEXT_LOCALE if (cookieLocale && LOCALES.includes(cookieLocale)) { locale = cookieLocale } } // 3) If still default and no cookie, detect from Accept-Language (only if URL didn't specify) const noCookie = !req?.cookies?.NEXT_LOCALE if (!hasUrlLocale && locale === 'es' && noCookie) { const detected = detectLocaleFromHeader(req?.headers?.['accept-language']) if (detected) locale = detected } // Set locale cookie for persistence const cookieMaxAge = 60 * 60 * 24 * 365 res.setHeader('Set-Cookie', `NEXT_LOCALE=${locale}; Path=/; Max-Age=${cookieMaxAge}; SameSite=Lax`) // ... } ``` ### Header: Nav Hrefs Must Prepend Locale Prefix After stripping any multiclient prefix (`/s/en/nexa-paraguay/`), prepend the current locale to every nav href. The language switcher must also be in the header — implement a dropdown with flag emoji + language code: ```tsx // In Header.tsx const LOCALE_FLAGS = { es: '🇪🇸 ES', en: '🇬🇧 EN', nl: '🇳🇱 NL', de: '🇩🇪 DE' } const currentLocale = locale || 'es' function switchLocale(newLocale: string) { const path = router.asPath const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] let cleanPath = path const parts = path.split('/').filter(Boolean) if (parts.length > 0 && LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { cleanPath = '/' + parts.slice(1).join('/') || '/' } window.location.href = '/' + newLocale + cleanPath } // Nav item href rendering let href = item.href || '#' // Strip existing multiclient prefix href = href.replace(/^\/s\/[^/]+\/[^/]+/, '') // Prepend locale prefix for internal links if (!href.startsWith('http') && !href.startsWith('/' + currentLocale)) { href = '/' + currentLocale + href } ``` ### Locale JSON Key Parity Verification Write a comparison script (`scripts/compare-locale-keys.py`) that walks key paths across all locale JSONs using dot-separated paths and finds truly missing keys (not structural differences). Add empty strings for missing keys: ```python import json def walk(obj, path=''): results = set() if isinstance(obj, dict): for k, v in obj.items(): p = f'{path}.{k}' if path else k results.add(p) results |= walk(v, p) elif isinstance(obj, list) and obj and isinstance(obj[0], dict): for i, item in enumerate(obj): results |= walk(item, path) else: results.add(path) return results ``` ### Catch-All Route [[...slug]] Instead of [slug] To support `/en/faq` style multi-segment URLs, use `[[...slug]]` NOT `[slug]`: ```bash mv src/pages/"[slug].tsx" src/pages/"[[...slug]].tsx" ``` This captures `/` → empty array, `/faq` → `['faq']`, `/en/faq` → `['en', 'faq']`. `index.tsx` CANNOT coexist — delete it and handle the home page case within `[[...slug]]`. ### Locale Extraction ```typescript export function getServerSideProps({ params }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } const fullContent = loadJSON(process.cwd() + '/content', `${locale}.json`) || ... } ``` ### Locale JSON Key Parity Before activating, audit all locale JSON files against the reference (`es.json`). Walk every key and check it exists in each locale. Add missing keys with empty strings (placeholders until translated). The gaps are typically 10-15 keys per locale: new pages not propagated, recently-added content keys, object sub-keys. ### Verification ```bash for loc in es en nl de; do for page in "" faq sobre servicios programas contacto blog privacidad glosario; do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://site.com/${loc}/${page}" done done ``` ## Asset Activation Policy Nothing gets deleted without client confirmation. Every dormant asset is an unactivated feature. Keep all `@ai-whisperers/*` packages installed (tree-shaken at build time, zero runtime cost). Keep unreferenced images on disk and in the manifest (pre-generated for future campaigns, <1MB total). #### Content JSON Integrity Check (Pre-Deploy) Before building the Docker image, validate ALL content JSON files. A corrupt JSON makes SSR return `content: {}` (empty pages) with zero errors in Docker logs: ```bash python3 -c " import json, os for f in os.listdir('content'): if f.endswith('.json'): try: json.load(open(f'content/{f}')) except json.JSONDecodeError as e: print(f'CORRUPT: {f} — {e}') exit(1) print('All content JSON valid') " ``` ### Stale .next/ directory pitfall After `git pull origin main` that changes content JSON files or source code, the existing `.next/` directory may be stale. The Docker `COPY . .` includes `.next/`, so stale build output + new content = empty SSR pages. Fix: ```bash rm -rf .next/ # Only when source/content files changed npm run build # Rebuild from scratch ``` After any content edit or page config change, validate all section content keys resolve: ```python import json, os with open('content/es.json') as f: content = json.load(f) for fname in os.listdir('nexa-pages'): cfg = json.load(open(f'nexa-pages/{fname}')) for sec in cfg.get('sections', []): ck = sec.get('content', sec['id']) resolved = content for part in ck.split('.'): if isinstance(resolved, dict) and part in resolved: resolved = resolved[part] else: resolved = None; break if resolved is None: print(f"MISSING: {fname} → {sec['id']} → {ck}") ``` ## Client Site Deep-Dive — Full Business Assessment When a client site already exists with docs (like Nexa Paraguay), don't just look at code — produce a structured assessment that covers ALL areas the business owner needs to see. ### Assessment Structure 1. **Code & Content Audit** — browse the live site + examine repo structure + read all docs 2. **Competitive Position** — price positioning, AI readiness, niche focus (use existing docs if they exist) 3. **Financial Model Review** — unit economics, funnel estimates, cost structure (which docs exist) 4. **Gap Analysis** — what's broken now (P0/P1) vs what needs client input 5. **Phased Roadmap** — organize into 6 phases by impact: (1) Fix broken pages, (2) Conversion path, (3) Trust & credibility, (4) SEO & analytics, (5) Multi-language, (6) AI differentiation ### Quick-Win Batch Sizing Always include a "quick-wins I can execute right now" section with concrete, batch-fixable items: - Fix page config `id` fields across all page JSONs - Add sitemap.xml + robots.txt - Replace fabricated stats with honest text - Fix blog image key mismatches - Remove `is_demo` flag - Add unique meta titles per page - Enable additional locale routing ### Business Model Impact Table Show the before/after for the client in financial terms they understand: | Metric | Current | With improvements | |---|---|---| | Clients/month | X | Y | | Avg ticket | $Z | $W | | Monthly revenue | $A | $B | ### Key Questions to Answer - What's the AI differentiation opportunity? (Check competitor landscape — most have zero AI) - What justifies the premium pricing? (AI layer, multilingual, faster process) - What's the head start window? (Usually 12-18 months before competitors catch up) - What are the 3-5 blog posts that would dominate zero-competition keywords? ## Pitfalls See `references/post-deployment-audit-pattern.md` for the systematic audit process to run after every deployment. - **Factory-demo content ships by default**: Fabricated stats (+500 families, 98% success), AI headshots, fake testimonials — these are NOT placeholders the client filled in, they're the scaffold. Replace them before anyone sees the site. Check `is_demo` flag in site.json.**: The `template-nextjs-client` has 52 files that must be removed for B2B. Missing any will cause build failures. Run the transformation checklist above. - **@ai-whisperers package versions**: Always resolve version mismatches before building. Use `GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token)` for npm install. - **Network name**: Always use `agent-net` for Swarm stacks. Verify with `docker network ls | grep overlay`. - **Build output**: The Next.js `output: 'standalone'` config is required for Docker builds. Set in `next.config.ts`. - **Blog data structure**: Template expects `blog.posts[]`. If your content has flat `blog[]`, adapt both `app/blog/page.tsx` and `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`. - **docker-compose.yml stale values**: The template's compose file has `superspuma` hardcoded in image name and Traefik labels. Replace ALL references. - **Content JSON has Fun4Me remnants**: The template pre-populates `content/es.json` with erotic/Fun4Me products, images, blog posts, brand references, Instagram handles (@fun4me_py), and a `brand` section. Overwrite completely with client content. Also check these files for hardcoded Fun4Me values: - `components/share-whatsapp.tsx` — hardcoded "Mirá esto de Fun4Me:" text - `components/article-json-ld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/breadcrumb-jsonld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/faq-json-ld.tsx` — reads `item.q`/`item.a` but content has `question`/`answer` - `app/rss.xml/route.ts` — "Fun4Me Store" title and "bienestar íntimo" description - `app/contacto/page.tsx` — Quick links for order status, returns, shipping; Instagram @fun4me_py; hardcoded Fun4Me phone - `app/nosotros/page.tsx` — "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section with sex shop story; reads `c.brand` instead of `c.nosotros` - **Dead index files**: After removing components, check `lib/hooks/index.ts` and similar index files for dangling re-exports. - **AnalyticsProvider API**: `@ai-whisperers/seo` v0.2.0 has a different API than the template expects. The `AnalyticsProvider` component from the package may be empty or mismatched. If build fails, remove the import and use raw GA4 script tag instead. - **DNS can't be configured from this environment**: Cloudflare API token not available. Must add A records through the Cloudflare web dashboard. - **No prices on B2B sites**: B2B products are custom-quoted. Never show prices. Use "Request quote" CTAs instead of "Buy now". - **WhatsApp as primary CTA**: For B2B in Paraguay, WhatsApp is the dominant business communication tool. Every page should have a WhatsApp float + at least one WhatsApp CTA section. - **Mobile sticky WhatsApp bar**: Add `components/mobile-cta.tsx` and include in layout. Add `pb-20 md:pb-0` to body className for spacing. - **Docker build with GITHUB_TOKEN**: Using `--build-arg GITHUB_TOKEN` triggers Docker BuildKit secret warnings and fails. Instead use `Dockerfile.prod` that copies the prebuilt `.next/standalone` directory and runs `node_modules/.bin/next start` — no npm install at Docker build time. Build on the host first, then Docker just packages output. - **CI/CD reusable workflow failures**: The `Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd` reusable workflows may not be accessible from new repos due to org permissions. Use a standalone `deploy.yml` that SSHes to the VPS and runs the deploy commands directly. This avoids the ci-cd access issue entirely. - **FAQ JSON-LD format mismatch**: If FAQ data uses `question`/`answer` keys but the JSON-LD component reads `q`/`a`, it silently fails to render FAQ schema. Fix by updating the component's key access. - **RSS feed inherits template owner**: The template's `app/rss.xml/route.ts` has hardcoded site name, description, and base URL. Always update all three for the new client. - **All JSON-LD components hardcode fun4me URL**: Every `*-json-ld.tsx` component needs its base URL updated. Read from `content.site.url` instead of hardcoding. {"action": "write_file", "file_content": "# PPR Implementation on Nexa Paraguay

## Context
Nexa Paraguay (Next.js 16 App Router, Tailwind v4, 4 locales, 128 routes) was migrated from fully-dynamic SSR to PPR (Partial Pre-Rendering) with `cacheComponents: true`.

## Files Changed

### next.config.js
```js
const nextConfig = {
  experimental: {
    ppr: true,
    cacheComponents: true,
  },
}
```

### Page Layout Pattern

The actual `[locale]/layout.tsx`:
```tsx
export const experimental_ppr = true
// NO revalidate, NO dynamic, NO dynamicParams at layout level
```

Pages use `generateStaticParams` to pre-render locale+slug combos at build time, then PPR serves the HTML shell instantly while client-only sections stream in via Suspense.

### Key Components

1. **`src/lib/page-data.ts`** — sync data loader (readFileSync from content JSON + page config JSON). Used by both `generateStaticParams` (async) and `generateMetadata` (must be sync).

2. **`src/app/[locale]/page.tsx`** — home page. Renders static HeroSection, trust section, etc. outside Suspense. Dynamic sections (intake wizard, testimonials, blog posts) in Suspense with Skeleton fallbacks.

3. **`src/app/[locale]/[slug]/page.tsx`** — all other pages. Same pattern: static shell + Suspense for dynamic content.

4. **`src/app/[locale]/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`** — blog detail pages. PPR render. Blog posts read from `content/blog/posts-{locale}.json` with `{ posts: [...] }` format.

### Migration Steps

1. Add `experimental.ppr: true` and `cacheComponents: true` to next.config.js
2. Add `export const experimental_ppr = true` to layout.tsx
3. Remove `revalidate`, `dynamic`, `dynamicParams` exports from all pages
4. Make `generateMetadata` synchronous (was async)
5. Wrap dynamic sections in `<Suspense fallback={<Skeleton />}>`
6. Replace all `new Date()` and other uncached data in server components
7. Ensure no `useRouter`/`headers()`/`cookies()` in static shell components
8. Verify build output shows `◐ Partial Prerender` for all routes

### Verification Script

```bash
npx next build | grep -E '(◐|○|ƒ)'
# ◐ = Partial Prerender (good)
# ○ = Static (good)  
# ƒ = Dynamic (only for API routes)
```

### PPR Traps Found

1. **`useRouter` in NavBar**: The navigation component used `useRouter` for active link detection. Breaks PPR static shell. Fix: pass locale as prop instead of using `useRouter().pathname`.

2. **Footer copyright year**: `{new Date().getFullYear()}` makes the entire page dynamic. Fix: hardcode the year.

3. **Blog posts data structure**: Some locales had `posts.json` with `{ posts: [...] }` instead of flat array. Load function must handle both.

4. **`generateMetadata` async fails silently**: The page renders but the build output shows `ƒ Dynamic`. Must be sync function.

5. **Skeleton size matters**: Heavy Skeletons with multiple nested divs defeat PPR's purpose. Keep fallbacks simple.

{"action": "write_file", "file_content": "# Client Content Honesty — No Fabricated Data

## The Rule

**Never ship fabricated stats on a client site.** This is the #1 client trust issue.

Factory-demo content from templates includes:
- "+500 families relocated"
- "98% success rate"
- "+47,000 residencias en 2025"
- "+63% crecimiento anual"
- "+189% comunidad holandesa"
- Fake testimonials with AI-generated headshots
- "Servicios a la carta" badges

## What the Client Said (Luana, Nexa Paraguay)

> "Like all that can just be like that's bullshit honestamente, que tipo not real, so I don't want to have something that's not true on the page."

This is a hard veto — if the client finds fabricated data on their live site, trust is damaged. Replace with:

## Safe Alternatives

| Fabricated stat | Replace with |
|----------------|--------------|
| "+500 families" | "8-12 weeks process" |
| "98% success" | "Fixed price" |
| "47k residencias" | "Personal support" |
| Any fake number | Process benefit (predictable, transparent, personal) |

## Content Audit Checklist

Before showing any client site:
1. ✅ Check all `trustBadges` arrays — remove any that aren't real
2. ✅ Check `stats` sections — replace placeholder values with honest text
3. ✅ Check testimonials — if no real clients yet, remove the section entirely or replace with a feedback form
4. ✅ Check `is_demo` flags in site.json
5. ✅ Check service/program pricing — confirm client wants prices shown (most B2B advisory doesn't)
6. ✅ Check team member profiles — remove fabricated names/headshots
7. ✅ Check success story numbers — only real client outcomes

## Feedback Section Pattern

When there are no real testimonials yet, replace with a simple feedback/form section:

```json
{
  "feedback": {
    "eyebrow": "TU OPINIÓN",
    "title": "Compartí tu experiencia",
    "subtitle": "¿Estás considerando mudarte a Paraguay? Contanos qué te gustaría saber.",
    "placeholder": "Escribí tu comentario o pregunta...",
    "buttonText": "Enviar"
  }
}
```

This is honest, builds engagement, and collects user-generated content for future testimonials.
